justification: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and thoroughly. Formal escalation is produced with explicit call for executive arbitration. No acceptance criteria are produced as if the conflict is resolved — AC-1 explicitly notes the policy is pending. confidence.level is 35, well under the 45 threshold. The proposed compromise (Option C) is clearly labeled as a recommendation to explore, not a resolution.

observations.text: Escalation is formal, detailed, and explicitly names executive leadership as the required decision-maker
BR-3 clearly states the visibility policy is UNRESOLVED and requires executive arbitration
Confidence level of 35 accurately reflects the unresolvable conflict
Options A-D are presented without selecting one as the answer
Acceptance criteria are parameterized on the unresolved policy rather than assuming a resolution
Identifies Legal/Compliance as an unconsidered stakeholder who must weigh in
rejection_reasons section reinforces that the spec cannot be implemented as stated
Edge cases (personal stops, batch delivery, screenshotting patterns) strengthen the case for why this needs careful resolution
